title: Stop extracting data: Run serverless Python natively in BigQuery!
summary: Google Cloud has made Python User Defined Functions (UDFs) generally available in BigQuery, allowing users to run custom Python code natively within SQL statements without infrastructure management. The feature supports importing libraries like BeautifulSoup and machine learning tokenizers for serverless execution.
writeup: The video covers the general availability of Python User Defined Functions in BigQuery, which run custom Python code directly inside SQL statements without any infrastructure to manage. This matters because it removes the need to extract data to a separate environment for Python processing, keeping computation next to the data and serverless. Key takeaways: the UDFs support importing libraries such as BeautifulSoup and machine learning tokenizers, and they enable local inference within BigQuery.
